game_name: Dragon Quest VI: Realms of Revelation

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinctly-designed enemy sprites with unique coloration and animation patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Story-critical threats that require party preparation and progression gates

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen transition to first-person battle view with enemy formation
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger present; player must engage or flee

danger_signals.visual_form: Darkened palette, changed lighting in environments
danger_signals.meaning: Certain dangerous story events and threats only manifest during night

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated sprite movement during combat, distinct attack poses
danger_signals.meaning: Threat clarity; dangerous entities telegraph intent through motion

safety_signals.visual_form: Architectural structures, NPCs present, shops and gathering places visible
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone with vendors, healing services, and story progression opportunities

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinctive abbey structure serving as class-change sanctuary
safety_signals.meaning: Safe character progression hub where party can change classes and develop
Standard world state; reduced special event triggers

safety_signals.visual_form: Party wagon visible on world map, character swap interface available
safety_signals.meaning: Ability to reorganize party indicates safe preparation/travel state

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Class emblems (Warrior, Martial Artist, Sage, etc.) indicated through sprite appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Party members visible in towns and on world map; class progression unlocked at Alltrades Abbey

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct monster sprite designs; Slime-type enemies serve as series-recognizable faction signature
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Secret Liquid Metal Slime class unlocked late-game indicates enemy faction integration into progression
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Random spawning in wilderness and dungeons; Monster Master class mechanics

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Conceptually distinct from Real World; separate but parallel map structure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Visually similar geography to Real World but with thematic variation; requires world-transition mechanics to access

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard medieval fantasy environment; castles, towns, wilderness with normal daylight/night cycle
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs follow standard routines; standard random encounters; story events progress normally

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Parallel map geometry with similar but distinct visual theming; suggests surreal/mystical interpretation of Real World locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different encounter tables; story-specific events tied to dream mechanics; character amnesia plot thread manifests through world transitions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Darkened environment palette; altered lighting on structures and terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Specific story events only trigger during night; implied danger increase; distinct encounter spawning

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character sprite changes reflect class progression; visual advancement from starter to hybrid to secret classes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New skills and spells learned; stat bonuses applied (Merchant income bonus, Sage MP discount); permanent stat increases from mastered hybrid classes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss monster encounters with unique, memorable sprite designs larger than standard enemies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Specific boss defeat required to proceed; story flags trigger major world changes or access new regions

environmental_storytelling.example: Real World and Dream World share identical map structures but with visual/thematic variations; communicates the surreal nature of the plot and character amnesia theme through environmental parallelism
Critical story events only occur at night; visual palette shift signals when narrative content will unlock, making time passage mechanically meaningful
Alltrades Abbey serves as sole location for class changes; architectural uniqueness and story focus signal its narrative importance as character development hub
Visual appearance changes through class advancement reflect character growth; player observes party transformation over playtime
Slime enemies appear across multiple games in series; visual recognition creates continuity and faction identity within Dragon Quest universe

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Animated attack sequences; unique sprite animation distinct from standard enemy patterns; signals heightened threat level
Basic attack animations; spawned through random encounter system; smaller, simplified sprite designs relative to bosses
Standard sprite appearance; limited skill pool; lower combat effectiveness until class progression begins
Sprite appearance reflects class identity; expanded skill pool; permanent stat bonuses; visual signaling of progression investment
Standard interactions available; shops open; story exposition available through dialogue
Altered dialogue trees; specific night-only story events trigger; some NPCs may be absent or in different locations
Visible as party travel asset; character swap interface available; signals safe/preparation mode

notes: Dragon Quest VI uses classical Dragon Quest visual language: first-person turn-based combat perspective, overhead world navigation, sprite-based character and enemy representation. The dual Real World/Dream World structure is communicated primarily through map geometry parallelism and palette/thematic variation rather than radical visual distinction. Danger/safety signals rely heavily on environmental context (towns vs. wilderness, day vs. night) and encounter type classification (boss vs. random). The class system creates visible progression through sprite changes and learned ability manifestation in battles. Amnesia plot device is environmental rather than explicitly visual—communicated through world structure and story progression gating. Mini-medals and arena/contest systems provide optional content signaling through NPC dialogue rather than environmental visual cues.
